perturbation.original_text: Subject to Distributor's performance of its obligations under this Agreement, Distributor is appointed as a NETGEAR Distributor and may purchase certain equipment ("Hardware") and licenses for software including revisions and updates ("Software"), as are listed in NETGEAR's then-current price list (the "Price List") for resale within the Territory (as defined below). During the initial one (1) year term of this Agreement. Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in the Territory, subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Distributor's performance of its obligations under this Agreement, Distributor is appointed as a NETGEAR Distributor and may purchase certain equipment ("Hardware") and licenses for software including revisions and updates ("Software"), as are listed in NETGEAR's then-current price list (the "Price List") for resale within the Territory (as defined below). During the initial one (1) year term of this Agreement. Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in the Territory, subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4. For the purposes of this provision, a reseller shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Distributor' and 'Reseller' are used seemingly interchangeably here. In Section 1, the agreement states that for the purposes of the Agreement, "distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale". This definition is replaced, however, changing the term 'distributor' to 'reseller' without updating the rest of the document to reflect this change. This creates an in-text contradiction, especially in sections where the term distributor is mentioned. By changing the definition of distributor to reseller it is now unclear what are each party's obligations and rights since distributor and reseller are two different legal entities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. PROMOTION AND SALE. Distributor shall sell or license Products only to resale customers which will in-turn resell or transfer the licenses to those Products to end use customers. Distributor may not sell or license Products directly to end use customers without the express written consent of NETGEAR. Distributor agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to maximize sales of NETGEAR Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'distributor' as 'reseller' but the contradicted text still uses the original definition of 'distributor', creating an inconsistency. This contradiction is significant because the contradicted text outlines the Distributor's responsibilities regarding sales and licensing of products to end use customers. By redefining 'distributor' as 'reseller,' it introduces confusion about who is responsible for those obligations outlined in the contradicted text. This directly aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise instructed by Distributor, NETGEAR will ship Products ordered by Distributor using the method and carrier specified in Distributor's then current Routing Guide, as may be amended from time to time by written notice from Distributor. The version of the Routing Guide which is in force on the Effective Date of this Agreement is attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 2. Distributor is responsible for all freight, handling, insurance and other transportation charges, and agrees to pay all such charges if separately identified on NETGEAR's invoice. NETGEAR will ship freight collect, uninsured, if so instructed by Distributor's order.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise instructed by Distributor, NETGEAR will ship Products ordered by Distributor using the method and carrier specified in Customer's then current Routing Guide, as may be amended from time to time by written notice from Customer. The version of the Routing Guide which is in force on the Effective Date of this Agreement is attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 2. Distributor is responsible for all freight, handling, insurance and other transportation charges, and agrees to pay all such charges if separately identified on NETGEAR's invoice. NETGEAR will ship freight collect, uninsured, if so instructed by Distributor's order.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the terms 'Distributor' and 'Customer' are used seemingly interchangeably, which are two different legal entities. This is creating confusion and it is unclear which legal entity's routing guide should be followed. By changing the legal entity that should be followed (distributor -> Customer), it creates a contradiction on who should be responsible for giving the intructions on where to ship the Products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise instructed by Distributor, NETGEAR will ship Products ordered by Distributor using the method and carrier specified in Customer's then current Routing Guide, as may be amended from time to time by written notice from Customer. The version of the Routing Guide which is in force on the Effective Date of this Agreement is attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 2. Distributor is responsible for all freight, handling, insurance and other transportation charges, and agrees to pay all such charges if separately identified on NETGEAR's invoice. NETGEAR will ship freight collect, uninsured, if so instructed by Distributor's order.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Distributor' to 'Customer' in specifying whose Routing Guide should be followed introduces a contradiction. The original text consistently refers to the 'Distributor' as the entity responsible for providing shipping instructions. By changing this to 'Customer,' the modified text creates ambiguity and direct conflict with the rest of the document, including the contradicted text, which assumes the Distributor's instructions. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract now uses two different legal entities ('Distributor' and 'Customer') interchangeably in a way that leads to conflicting obligations regarding shipping instructions.

perturbation.original_text: In the advertising and promotion of the Products, Distributor agrees to use NETGEAR's and certain of Bay Networks' trade names, logos and trademarks (the "Trademarks") as reasonably instructed by NETGEAR during the term of the Agreement. Solely for this purpose, NETGEAR and Bay Networks grant Distributor a non-exclusive, royalty-free, limited right to use the Trademarks. Distributor will not make or permit the removal or modification of any Trademarks or tags, proprietary notices, labels, or other identifying marks placed by Bay Networks, NETGEAR or their agents on the Products or associated literature.
perturbation.changed_text: In the advertising and promotion of the Products, Partner agrees to use NETGEAR's and certain of Bay Networks' trade names, logos and trademarks (the "Trademarks") as reasonably instructed by NETGEAR during the term of the Agreement. Solely for this purpose, NETGEAR and Bay Networks grant Partner a non-exclusive, royalty-free, limited right to use the Trademarks. Partner will not make or permit the removal or modification of any Trademarks or tags, proprietary notices, labels, or other identifying marks placed by Bay Networks, NETGEAR or their agents on the Products or associated literature.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Distributor' to 'Partner,' it is creating contradiction on who has the rights to use NETGEAR's trademarks, since the contract starts off by indicating distributor and NETGEAR are the parties involved, but this section mentions partner which is a different legal entity that wasn't mentioned anywhere in the text. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract is not clear on which specific entity has the right to use these trademarks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. USE OF TRADEMARKS. In the advertising and promotion of the Products, Partner agrees to use NETGEAR's and certain of Bay Networks' trade names, logos and trademarks (the "Trademarks") as reasonably instructed by NETGEAR during the term of the Agreement. Solely for this purpose, NETGEAR and Bay Networks grant Partner a non-exclusive, royalty-free, limited right to use the Trademarks. Partner will not make or permit the removal or modification of any Trademarks or tags, proprietary notices, labels, or other identifying marks placed by Bay Networks, NETGEAR or their agents on the Products or associated literature.
perturbation.justification: The original text changed 'Distributor' to 'Partner'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The explanation states that the change creates a contradiction because it's unclear which entity has rights to trademarks. However, since the contradicted text and changed text are identical, there's no new contradiction created by the changed text that wasn't already present in the contradicted text. The problem already existed, and the change didn't make it worse. Therefore, there's no *new* in-text contradiction introduced by the change. If the contradicted text had referenced the distributor, then the change would cause a contradiction. Since the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, there is no contradiction.
